spec_match.rationale: Starts a `getDisplayMedia` capture, then calls the new `CaptureController` surface — forwardWheel / setZoomLevel — directly. That is exactly the API the spec adds for capturer-side control.

interactive.rationale: Working start-capture button + 7 listeners on synthetic scroll/zoom controls with an action log that reflects each call's resolution. Real working flow with feature-detect probe.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `Start a tab capture, then send synthetic scroll and zoom commands ... Every call returns whether the underlying surface accepted it`. The action log delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Action log surfaces individual call outcomes, but the no-capture case (calling forwardWheel before getDisplayMedia resolves) and the permission-denied / user-clicks-decline case are not explicit demos.

references.rationale: `see also` cites the chromestatus link and the WICG captured-surface-control spec.

design_system.rationale: 44 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Cover the permission-denied / pre-capture call path
openQuestions.detail: If the user clicks `cancel` on the share picker, the subsequent `forwardWheel`/`setZoomLevel` calls should reject. Surface that error path explicitly so the API's contract is visible — not just the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Show the unsupported-surface case
openQuestions.detail: Not every captured surface accepts forward-wheel (window or screen captures may not). Either preselect tab-only via `displaySurface: 'browser'` in the options or surface the rejection when the user picks an unsupported surface — right now both branches look identical.

summary: Correct umbrella demo for the three legs of Captured Surface Control with real `CaptureController` calls, a working action log, and an explicit Chrome 136 origin-trial/flag banner. The permission-denied and unsupported-surface paths still need explicit treatment for a complete portrait of the API's contract.
